content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the HEAD change against `origin/master` outside `.ralph-burning`. Found 1 high-severity correctness gap: `try_reconcile_success` now fails closed for a missing binding, but `persist_workflow_run_id` still guesses the latest `RunStarted`, which can silently create the same wrong-run reconciliation the bead is meant to prevent.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: The patch still lets the daemon guess a run identity in `persist_workflow_run_id`, so a manual rerun between dispatch completion and persistence can stamp the newer run onto the task and bypass the new fail-closed reconciliation guard.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1779-1810
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1941-1944
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:7797-7948
remediation_hint: Stop deriving `workflow_run_id` from the latest `RunStarted` event. Persist the authoritative run id from the dispatch/result path or another deterministic artifact of the just-finished attempt; if that binding cannot be proven, leave the field unset so `try_reconcile_success` takes the new `reconciliation_no_run_binding` path.
   Rationale: Lines 1800-1810 explicitly acknowledge that this code may bind the wrong run_id if another run starts before persistence. The new guard at lines 1941-1944 only fails closed when `workflow_run_id` is missing; once the wrong id has been written, reconciliation will trust it and can still close/sync the bead and rewrite milestone lineage for the wrong attempt. The added regression test only covers the `workflow_run_id == None` branch, so this remaining unsafe path is untested.
